title: Boston to ‘board the Sue Sylvester Express’ as Jane Lynch, alongside Megan Hilty, are added to Boston Pops July 4 show
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News “Our blockbuster lineup for July 4 just got even more spectacular with the addition of Jane Lynch and Megan Hilty,” Boston Pops conductor Keith Lockhart said.
Jane Lynch attended the Critics Choice Association's third annual Celebration of LGBTQ+ Cinema & Television at the Four Seasons Hotel in Los Angeles on May 29.

VALERIE MACON By Carson Lyle June 16, 2026 | 7:15 PM 1 minute to read Emmy and Golden Globe winner Jane Lynch is scheduled to host the annual Boston Pops Firework Spectacular on July 4, while Tony Award winner Megan Hilty has been added to the cast of performers, the organization announced.

title: Skeletal remains of 3 people found in foreclosed Conn. home
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News The new home buyer purchased the house “as is” at a foreclosure auction and later found the three bodies inside.
By Darin Zullo June 16, 2026 | 6:53 PM 1 minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.
Connecticut State Police are investigating after the skeletal remains of three bodies were found inside a foreclosed home on Sunday.

title: Patriots, Kraft Group sue Foxborough over annual stadium licensing fees
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New England Patriots Plaintiffs allege town officials imposed nearly $1 million in new charges tied to the Gillette Stadium events.
Robert Kraft, owner of the Patriots, at the NFL Super Bowl in February.

AP Photo/Frank Franklin II Kraft Sports & Entertainment and the New England Patriots have filed a lawsuit against the Town of Foxborough over new financial obligations tied to Gillette Stadium’s entertainment license.

title: Celtics columnist: Miami has the edge in Giannis Antetokounmpo chase
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "I don't think he's going to be a Celtic and that's just from what I'm hearing," The Boston Globe's Gary Washburn said.
Bucks' Giannis Antetokounmpo warming up before a game in March, 2026.
AP Photo/Aaron Gash As the deadline for a potential Giannis Antetokounmpo trade draws nearer, the Celtics have frequently come up as a possible suitor.

title: 3 New England winners take home top honors at the 2026 James Beard Awards
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Food News Providence's Loma, Portland restaurateur Dana Street, and Dover chef Evan Hennessey earned top honors at the restaurant industry's biggest awards night.
Providence, RI.

07/09/24 - Champagne-style cocktail with red beet sugar cube, amaro sweet vermouth, and sparkling wine at LOMA, a Latin-American influenced bar and lounge close to Depasquale Plaza off Atwells Avenue in Providence.

title: 5 takeaways from Norway’s World Cup win over Iraq at Boston Stadium, as Erling Haaland got off to a soaring start
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: World Cup After trading early scores, Norway slowly built a three-goal margin of victory.
Norway fans row during the World Cup matchup with Iraq.
AP Photo/Mark Stockwell Norway defeated Iraq 4-1 in the second World Cup game hosted at “Boston Stadium” (Gillette Stadium) on a sunny Tuesday afternoon in front of a crowd of 63,106.

title: Celebrations spill onto streets of Brockton after Cape Verde’s surprise World Cup draw
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News Rowdy crowds led to at least nine arrests following Monday’s match, according to Brockton police.
Cape Verde fans celebrate their country's tie with Spain on Monday in Brockton.

Suzanne Kreiter/Boston Globe Staff By Abby Patkin June 16, 2026 | 3:11 PM 1 minute to read As excited Cape Verde fans spilled out onto Brockton’s streets Monday to celebrate their soccer team’s stunning World Cup draw against Spain, local police say some revelers turned a little too rowdy.

title: 5 arrested after fights in downtown Boston restaurant
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Crime It's the same restaurant where police broke up an after-hours party attended by multiple Patriots players earlier this year.
Estella Restaurant in downtown Boston.
Finn Gomez for The Boston Globe Five people were arrested Monday after a large fight at a restaurant near Downtown Crossing, according to police.
